revision.text:
[[ja:茜社]] {{Infobox religious building |名称 = {{ill|Akane Shrine|ja|茜社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akane Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akane Shrine|qq=}} |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} |image = File:Akone-yashiro.jpg |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}{{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|qid=Q328067|lt=Ise City|1=Ise, Mie}}{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|1=Toyokawachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17223204}}274番地 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 29 |緯度秒 = 10.6 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 |経度秒 = 26.2 |地図 = Japan Mie |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Oshikumone|ja|天牟羅雲命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshikumone|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshikumone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天押雲根命|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天押雲根命}}
{{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒルコ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒルコ|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1067028}} |type = 旧村社
{{ill|Yamada Santoshin Shrines|ja|山田産土神八社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Santoshin Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Santoshin Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |創建 = In the era of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|en|Emperor Ichijō|qid=Q449540|lt=Ichijō Tennō|1=Emperor Ichijō}} (prior to {{ill|986|en|986|qid=Q23785|lt=986 AD|1=986}}) |本殿 = |別名 = {{ill|Akone-san|ja|あこねさん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akone-san|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akone-san|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |札所等 = |festival = {{ill|July 15|en|July 15|qid=Q2686|lt=July 15|1=July 15}} |神事 = {{ill|Mitokami Ritual|ja|御頭神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitokami Ritual|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitokami Ritual|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}（{{ill|January 15|en|January 15|qid=Q2260|lt=January 15|1=January 15}}） |location = {{ill|Toyokawachō|qid=Q17223204|1=Toyokawachō}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.486278|N|136.707278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Akane Shrine''' (茜社, あこねやしろ) is a Shinto shrine enshrined in {{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|1=Toyokawachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17223204}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|qid=Q328067|lt=Ise City|1=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is one of the {{ill|Yamada Santoshin Shrines|ja|山田産土神八社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Santoshin Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Santoshin Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; under the modern shrine Ranking system its former Rank (社格) was that of a village shrine (村社)宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：930 - 931ページ. Although it is located within the precincts of the {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Jingū|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} (the Outer Shrine, [[:en:Toyōke Daijingū|Toyōke Daijingū]]), it is not administered by Ise Jingū櫻井（1991）：273 - 274ページ. Local residents affectionately refer to it as “Akone-san”{{ill|Ise Shima Keizai Shimbun|ja|伊勢志摩経済新聞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Shima Keizai Shimbun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Shima Keizai Shimbun|1=ISESHIMA KEIZAI SHIMBUN|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q18700592}} "[http://iseshima.keizai.biz/headline/1541/ Ise’s First Female Priest – the Water Deity Akane Shrine, Endearingly Called 'Akone-san']" 2012nd year 10月5日 (accessed 2013rd year 10月12日). == Overview == The shrine’s precincts cover 601 tsubo (approximately 1,987 m2) and are bordered on the east, south, and west by the Magatama Pond (まがたまいけ) within the Outer Shrine’s sacred grounds宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：933ページ. The name “Akone” derives from the old local place name {{ill|Akau-ne|ja|赤畝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akau-ne|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akau-ne|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which once surrounded the shrine’s location宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：930ページ. Within the precincts of Akane Shrine also stand two subsidiary shrines: {{ill|Toyokawa Akane Inari Shrine|ja|豊川茜稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa Akane Inari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa Akane Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 茜社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 茜社|1=Akane Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17191164}} and {{ill|Tenjin Shrine (Akane Ushitengami)|ja|天神社（茜牛天神）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine (Akane Ushitengami)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine (Akane Ushitengami)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The priest (宮司) has been {{ill|Mizuho Suzuki|en|Mizuho Suzuki|qid=Q3543908|lt=Suzuki Mizuho|1=Mizuho Suzuki}} since October 1, 2012 ({{ill|2012|en|2012|qid=Q1990|lt=Heisei 24|1=2012}}); Suzuki is the first female priest to serve a shrine in Ise City. == Beliefs == The parish (氏子) area covers {{ill|Toyokawa Town|ja|豊川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa Town|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa Town|1=Toyokawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11633645}}, {{ill|Fujisato, Akita|en|Fujisato, Akita|qid=Q1206039|lt=Fujisato Town|1=Fujisato, Akita}}, and {{ill|Asahi Town|ja|旭町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Town|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Town|1=Asahichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10313777}}. Along the approach (参道) to the main shrine building, numerous torii stand in line, and countless flags, banners, and fox figurines—offered by devotees—fill the precincts, attesting to the depth of local faith{{Cite news|url=http://www.ise-cci.or.jp/yeg/machi/omairi/geku/gegu.html|title=外宮|publisher={{ill|Ise Chamber of Commerce|ja|伊勢商工会議所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Chamber of Commerce|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Chamber of Commerce|1=Ise Shōkō Kaigisho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11378972}}|accessdate=2013rd year 10月12日|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1012-1938-41/www.ise-cci.or.jp/yeg/machi/omairi/geku/gegu.html|archivedate=2013rd year 10月12日|qq=}}. The writer {{ill|Aiko Satō (writer)|ja|佐藤愛子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiko Satō (writer)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiko Satō (writer)|1=Aiko Satō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11384027}} has featured Akane Shrine in her books 『私の遺言』 and 『冥途のお客』, making it a hidden landmark visited by diviners and ritual specialists{{ill|Ise Shima Keizai Shimbun|ja|伊勢志摩経済新聞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Shima Keizai Shimbun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Shima Keizai Shimbun|1=ISESHIMA KEIZAI SHIMBUN|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q18700592}} "[http://iseshima.keizai.biz/headline/621/ The 'Ushitengami' at Akone-san by the Magatama Pond of Ise Jingū’s Outer Shrine is the God of Scholarship]" 2009th year 1月1日 (accessed 2013rd year 10月12日). Festivals include the Mitokami Ritual on {{ill|January 15|en|January 15|qid=Q2260|lt=January 15|1=January 15}} and the annual festival on {{ill|July 15|en|July 15|qid=Q2686|lt=July 15|1=July 15}}. == Enshrined Deities == The principal deities enshrined are {{ill|Ame-no-Oshikumone|lt=Ameno Murakumo-no-Mikoto|ja|天牟羅雲命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshikumone|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshikumone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天押雲根命|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天押雲根命}} (あめのむらくものみこと) and {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒルコ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒルコ|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1067028}} (ひるこのみこと). According to the {{ill|Ujiyamada City History|ja|宇治山田市史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada City History|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada City History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it is recorded as "of unknown grouping; some say [it is that of] the ancestral deity of the Akau-ne clan, Ameno Murakumo-no-Mikoto". Hiruko-no-Mikoto was enshrined later through a merger, and Ameno Murakumo-no-Mikoto is regarded as the deity of drinking water, said to have brought water down from the heavens. == Subsidiary Shrines == Within the precincts are two subsidiary shrines: {{ill|Toyokawa Akane Inari Shrine|ja|豊川茜稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa Akane Inari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa Akane Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 茜社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 茜社|1=Akane Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17191164}} and {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine|1=Tenjinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11443279}}. === Toyokawa Akane Inari Shrine === Its founding is unknown, but it has long been enshrined within the precincts of Akane Shrine宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：931 - 932ページ. In the "Shōko Shū" account of Akane Shrine, there is mention that "to the northeast of the stone platform (祭壇) there is a rock cave dedicated to Inari, and it is said to be the local deity of Toyokawa"宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：931ページ. Its enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|qid=Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|1=Ukanomitama}} (うかのみたまのかみ). According to the "Shinto Meishōshi", the faith of local fishermen in this deity is particularly strong宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：932ページ. === Tenjin Shrine === Tenjin Shrine enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}}. Originally the tutelary shrine of the Yamada Ōji family, it later became a revered shrine of Toyokawa Town. It is also known as "Ushitengami"; there are two theories regarding the origin of this name. # One theory suggests that the tutelary deity's name morphed into "Ushigami." # Another posits that it derives from relocating the statue of Sugawara no Michizane from the Outer Shrine precinct—originally enshrined as a ward against the demon gate (鬼門) by the Yamada Ōji family—to a site where Ushigami (牛鬼) was venerated. Since Sugawara no Michizane is revered as the deity of scholarship, many exam candidates visit Tenjin Shrine each year. == Merged Shrines == On {{ill|February 19, 1909|ja|2月19日, 1909年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 19, 1909|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 19, 1909|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Meiji 42), Akane Shrine merged two shrines宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：930, 932ページ: {| class="wikitable" style="background-color:#fee0fb; width:40%" |- ! {{ill|Shrine Name|ja|社名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Name|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Name|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 商号|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 商号|1=Trade name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1417728}} !! {{ill|Enshrined Deity|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrined Deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrined Deity|1=Saijin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11591100}} !! {{ill|Former Location|ja|旧所在地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Location|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Location|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} !! {{ill|Remarks|ja|備考|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Remarks|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Remarks|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- | {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|蛭子社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} || {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒルコ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒルコ|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1067028}} || rowspan="2"| {{ill|Toyokawa Town|ja|豊川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa Town|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa Town|1=Toyokawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11633645}} {{ill|Shimobasho|ja|下馬所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimobasho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimobasho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} || |- | {{ill|Ōji Sugawara Shrine|ja|大路菅原社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji Sugawara Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji Sugawara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} || {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}} || Becomes the subsidiary shrine "Tenjin Shrine" (天神社) |} == History == The shrine is said to have been founded before the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|en|Emperor Ichijō|qid=Q449540|lt=Ichijō Tennō|1=Emperor Ichijō}} (i.e. prior to {{ill|986|en|986|qid=Q23785|lt=986 AD|1=986}}){{Cite web|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E8%8C%9C%E7%A4%BE/|title=茜社 - Mie Prefecture Shrine Agency Teaching Committee|publisher=Mie Prefecture Shrine Agency Teaching Committee|accessdate=|language=ja|qq=}}. In ancient times it was enshrined as a subsidiary shrine (摂社) of the Outer Shrine (外宮) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Jingū|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}, but over time it evolved into a tutelary deity (産土神). It was formerly known as "Akane-sha" or "Akau-ne Myōjin"{{Cite web|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E8%8C%9C%E7%A4%BE/|title=茜社 - Mie Prefecture Shrine Agency Teaching Committee|publisher=Mie Prefecture Shrine Agency Teaching Committee|accessdate=|language=ja|qq=}}. Until the 【Bunka】 and 【Bunsei】 periods there was only a stone platform (石壇) and no shrine building; during that time a small 【祠】 was first constructed{{Cite web|url=|title=|publisher=|accessdate=|qq=}}. The name "Akane-sha" is presumed to have come into use from the Edo Period through the Meiji Period. On {{ill|1871|en|1871|qid=Q7746|lt=Meiji 4|1=1871}} {{ill|8=ja|9=11月2日 (旧暦)|November 2 (Lunisolar)|lt=Lunisolar November 2|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}}: {{ill|1971|en|1971|qid=Q2475|lt=1971|1=1971}} {{ill|December 13|en|December 13|qid=Q2379|lt=December 13|1=December 13}}), it was classified as a village shrine (村社). In {{ill|1872|en|1872|qid=Q7753|lt=Meiji 5|1=1872}} in June (Gregorian: {{ill|1872|en|1872|qid=Q7753|lt=1872|1=1872}} July), the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|qid=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}} removed shrines not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} and {{ill|Enryaku Ritual Records|ja|延暦儀式帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku Ritual Records|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku Ritual Records|1=Enryaku Gishikichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q109596938}} from the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Jingū|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}, transferring them to the jurisdiction of {{ill|Dokaido Prefecture|ja|度会県|zh|度會縣|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|1=Watarai Prefecture|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dokaido Prefecture|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dokaido Prefecture|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11486130}} and {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}; as a result, Akane-sha was separated from the jurisdiction of Ise Jingū{{Cite news|url=http://www.ise-cci.or.jp/yeg/machi/omairi/geku/gegu.html|title=外宮|publisher={{ill|Ise Chamber of Commerce|ja|伊勢商工会議所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Chamber of Commerce|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Chamber of Commerce|1=Ise Shōkō Kaigisho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11378972}}|accessdate=2013rd year 10月12日|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1012-1938-41/www.ise-cci.or.jp/yeg/machi/omairi/geku/gegu.html|archivedate=2013rd year 10月12日|qq=}}. Meanwhile, Ise Jingū began discreet negotiations with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en|Ōyamazumi Shrine|qid=Q703904|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}}—located within the Inner Shrine precinct—to restore its jurisdiction over Akane-sha{{Cite news|url=|title=|publisher=|accessdate=|language=ja|qq=}}. This was because both shrines are located near the center of the sacred precinct and the sight of red and white banners and dilapidated torii was detracting from the overall landscape. Although the parishioners of Akane-sha did not consent to returning to Ise Jingū’s jurisdiction, Ōyama-tsumi Shrine, upon request from parishioners, along with the subsidiary {{ill|Koyasu Shrine|ja|子安神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine|qq=}}, returned to Ise Jingū’s jurisdiction. According to Sakurai Katsunoshin, the reason for Akane-sha not returning may have been due to the flourishing of Inari worship within its precincts櫻井（1991）：274ページ. In {{ill|1952|en|1952|qid=Q5272|lt=Showa 27|1=1952}} ({{ill|1952|en|1952|qid=Q5272|lt=1952|1=1952}}), it obtained the status of a religious corporation and became completely independent from Ise Jingū. On {{ill|May 25, 2007|ja|2007th year 5月25日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 25, 2007|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 25, 2007|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, during one of the ceremonies of the {{ill|Ise Jingū Shikinen Sengu|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Jingū Shikinen Sengu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Jingū Shikinen Sengu|qq=}}, the "Kamisage Kuruma" (a palanquin) carrying the sacred object was set in motion from Akane-sha by the Toyoe-kai"「上せ車」の白竜お披露目 伊勢"{{ill|The Asahi Shimbun|en|The Asahi Shimbun|qid=Q720503|lt=Asahi Shimbun|1=The Asahi Shimbun}} 2007th year 5月14日付 (Morning Edition, Mie Edition, p.23). == Treasures == Among its treasures, the shrine owns one lion head, two swords, one small sword, and one ox statue{{Cite news|url=http://www.ise-cci.or.jp/yeg/machi/omairi/geku/gegu.html|title=外宮|publisher={{ill|Ise Chamber of Commerce|ja|伊勢商工会議所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Chamber of Commerce|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Chamber of Commerce|1=Ise Shōkō Kaigisho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11378972}}|accessdate=2013rd year 10月12日|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1012-1938-41/www.ise-cci.or.jp/yeg/machi/omairi/geku/gegu.html|archivedate=2013rd year 10月12日|qq=}}. The lion head bears an inscription reading "阿古称 永禄二歳己未五月 度会元貞 南都宿院作" and on {{ill|April 27, 1982|ja|1982nd year 4月27日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 27, 1982|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 27, 1982|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it was designated as a tangible cultural property (sculpture) by {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}{{Cite news|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?data=%8E%82%8Eq&prePageNum=1&mngnum=0000524|title=みんなで、守ろう！活かそう！三重の文化財/獅子頭 阿古祢永禄二歳己未五月度会元貞南都宿院作の銘がある|publisher={{ill|Mie Prefecture Education Committee|ja|三重県教育委員会事務局社会教育・文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture Education Committee|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture Education Committee|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2013rd year 10月11日|qq=}}. == Access == ; Public Transportation * About a 5-minute walk from the south exit of {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|qid=Q6079748|lt=Ise City Station|1=Iseshi Station}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|qid=Q872023|lt=JR Sangu Line|1=Sangū Line}}. * About a 5-minute walk from the bus stop "Ise City Ekimae" of {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|qid=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kōtsū|1=Mie Kotsu}}. * Immediately adjacent to the {{ill|Gaiku-mae Bus Stop|ja|外宮前|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gaiku-mae Bus Stop|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gaiku-mae Bus Stop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Mie Kōtsū. ; By Car * About a 5-minute drive north via the {{ill|Ise Expressway|en|Ise Expressway|qid=Q6079578|lt=Ise Expressway|1=Ise Expressway}} and {{ill|Ise Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Nishi Interchange|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Nishi Interchange|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢西インターチェンジ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢西インターチェンジ|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10884865}} on {{ill|Mie Prefecture Route 32, Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture Route 32, Ise Isobe Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture Route 32, Ise Isobe Line|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11357920}} (御木本道路). Parking is available near the Outer Shrine area. == Footnotes == {{Reflist|2|qq=}} == References == * {{Cite book|language=ja|last=宇治山田市役所 編|title=『宇治山田市史 下巻』|publisher=宇治山田市役所|date=昭和4th year 3月5日|page=1690|qq=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|last=櫻井勝之進|title=『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』|publisher=国書刊行会|date=平成3rd year 11月30日|page=318|isbn=4-336-03296-3|qq=}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines in Japan|en|List of Shinto shrines in Japan|qid=Q2517815|lt=List of Shinto shrines in Japan|1=List of Shinto shrines in Japan}} == External Links == * [https://ise-akonesan.com/ Akone-san, Toyokawa Akane Inari Shrine] {{translated page|ja|茜社|version=94087419|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:茜社]] {{wikidata link|Q17191164}} [[Category:Son-sha in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:三重県指定有形文化財]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]]
